BetaTree 1.0.0
  • Reference
    • Creating a Beta Tree Histogram
    • Mode Hunting Using Beta-Trees

Set significance level for regions at each tree depth

ConfLevel.Rd

Computes the significance level at each tree depth.

Usage

ConfLevel(nd, alpha, method)

Arguments

nd

A vector. Number of bounded regions at every depth (starting at depth 0).

alpha

Numeric. The significance level (of the average density) of all the regions sums to \(\alpha\).

method

Use method = "bonferroni" or method = "weighted_bonferroni" method to adjust for multiple hypothesis testing.

Value

A vector of significance level \(\alpha_D\) at every tree depth (starting at depth = 0)

Details

The root node automatically has significance level 0. If method = "bonferroni", the significance level at depth d is $$ \hat{\alpha}_d = \frac{\alpha}{(d_\max - d_\min + 1) * n_d}, d\geq d_\min, $$ where \(d_\max\) is the maximum tree depth, \(d_\min\) is the minimum depth (greater than 0) where there are bounded regions, and \(n_d\) is number of bounded regions at depth \(d\).

If method = weighted_bonferroni, the confidence level at depth d is $$ \hat{\alpha}_d = \frac{\alpha}{(d_\max - d + 2) n_d \sum_{B=2}^{d_\max - d_\min + 2}1/B},d\geq d_\min. $$

On this page
